prose:
**Formaldehyde Fukui Reactivity Indices** — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted), PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Input geometry from user-supplied .xyz file, used as-is. Mulliken partial charges computed on neutral, cation (+1, ²A′), and anion (−1, ²A″) to yield Fukui indices. All SCF calculations converged. 

**Key findings:** The **carbon atom is the most electrophilic** (f⁺ = 0.708), with positive dual descriptor (+0.596) indicating it is locally soft and susceptible to nucleophilic attack. The **oxygen atom is the most nucleophilic** (f⁻ = 0.467), reflecting its lone-pair character. Hydrogen atoms show minimal reactivity (f⁰ ≈ 0.06). Fukui charge conservation verified. Results are consistent with formaldehyde's known electrophilic carbonyl reactivity toward nucleophiles. Spatial reactivity map available in the output directory.</prose>
<parameter name="integrity_trustworthy">true</integrity_trustworthy>
<parameter name="n_atoms">4
